hero.tagline: Planting trees of peace on a former minefield
hero.desc:
At the DMZ border, people from around the world plant peace flowering trees,
and take part in sustainable tourism experiencing Korean culture.

about.label: About
about.title: Why plant trees in the DMZ?
about.subtitle: Planting symbols of peace with your own hands on a site of global conflict. That is how PEACEWAY began.

partner1.name: K-MIND
partner1.desc: Overall planning, online platform, DMZ farm ownership and management

partner2.name: Cherry Blossom Village
partner2.desc: Offline experiences, local produce, cultural programs

partner3.name: Korea Mine Research Institute
partner3.desc: Mine education & experiences, overall safety management

about.calloutBody: A symbolic space leaving “peaceful footsteps” on DMZ land shaped like footprints from above—inside the Civilian Control Line where access is tightly limited. People from around the world plant trees here.
about.spaceBody:
DMZ Peace Footsteps Farm — Located in Paju, Gyeonggi-do, inside the Civilian Control Line. This farmland operates with military approval and is a special restricted-access area. About 30 minutes by car from central Paju.

Cherry Blossom Village — A rural experience venue in Paju. A cozy village space where cultural programs such as K-bibimbap and mother-of-pearl craft are held.

programs.label: Programs
programs.title: 🌳 Experience programs
programs.subtitle: A world-unique peace tourism experience only possible in the DMZ.
programs.treeLead: World-unique—plant flowering trees directly on DMZ land. Your tree is registered online and growth can be tracked.
programs.treeCalloutHtml:
Three reasons this program is special
🌍 World-unique — Plant trees directly on DMZ land
📱 Permanent record — Track your tree’s growth online
🇰🇷 Historic meaning — Inter-Korean summit tree (golden rain) planted in the DMZ
programs.mineLead: Grasp the reality of division through safe replica mines and metal detectors.
programs.mineWarnHtml: ⚠️ Safety: Only inert training replicas are used. Partner: Korea Mine Research Institute.
programs.mineTipHtml: 💡 Flow: Right after peace history → tree planting next to it (wishing for global peace).
programs.culture1p: Make bibimbap with Paju produce; pick your own fresh ingredients.
programs.culture2p: Cook and taste dishes such as faux-meat rice and corn rice cakes—understanding division through food.
programs.culture3p: Phone cases, key rings, hand mirrors with traditional mother-of-pearl.

journey.label: Journey
journey.title: 🗺️ Visitor journey
journey.subtitle: A day in the DMZ—planting peace.
journey.s2p: DMZ history + detector practice + replica observation
journey.s5p: Tree info + peace message registered online
journey.s6p: Follow your tree after returning home (phase 2 updates)
journey.totalHtml: 🕐 Total time: ~4–5 hours  |  Meet-up: designated Paju location  |  Bring: comfortable clothes, hat

garden.label: Peace Garden
garden.title: 🌸 Peace flowering trees
garden.subtitle: Trees planted in the DMZ peace garden—each carries a story of peace.
garden.tree2peace: National flower of Korea—resolve for peace
garden.tree6peace: Tree of peace and good harvest
garden.ctaHtml: 💡 Visiting soon? Check which trees can be planted and what is blooming—then book a program.

contact.label: Contact
contact.title: 📬 Inquiry & booking
contact.subtitle: Ask about programs or book. We aim to reply within 2 business days.

form.name: Name *
form.footnote: We will email you within 2 business days

contact.cardMapHtml:
Paju, Gyeonggi (Civilian Control Line area)
Details after booking is confirmed
contact.faq1a: Yes—the program welcomes international visitors. Advance booking and ID checks are required due to the Civilian Control Line.
contact.faq2a: Basic English support is provided; professional interpreters can be arranged for groups. AI live interpreting is planned.
contact.faq3q: Q. How much does it cost?
contact.faq3a: Fees depend on program mix and group size; we send a quote via the inquiry form.
contact.faq4q: Q. Is the mine exercise safe?
contact.faq4a: Yes—only inert training replicas, supervised by Korea Mine Research Institute experts.
contact.faq5q: Q. Can I see my tree later?
contact.faq5a: Yes—once the online tracking phase launches, you can follow your tree with photos and location data.
contact.faq6a: Yes—welcome for 10+ guests; tailored options for companies and schools.
contact.faq7a: Light rain: we provide rain gear; severe weather: we reschedule.
contact.faq9a: Designated lots near the site—details after booking.
contact.faq10q: Q. How do we get there?
contact.faq10a: About 30 minutes by car from central Paju; entry requires prior approval—we send directions when you book. Shuttles possible for groups.

patent.label: Technology
patent.title: 🌐 AI interpreting tourism platform
patent.subtitle: Innovation from PEACEWAY PAJU—a future of peace tourism without language barriers
patent.patentEn: Method and system for providing tourism information to foreign tourists in two directions using a group chat platform equipped with artificial intelligence interpretation and translation functions
patent.calloutGoldHtml: The world’s first group-tourism interpreting system where one guide serves many nationalities in their own languages via AI two-way interpreting.
patent.f1p: Guide’s Korean is converted to text + voice in each guest’s language
patent.f2p: Guests chat in their languages with live mutual translation
patent.f3p: Self-improving quality using AI engines such as GPT and Gemini
patent.f4p: Smartphone + earbuds for each guest’s language
patent.downloadAria: App Store for iOS and Play Store for Android
patent.downloadLinkTitleApp: App Store link for iOS — coming soon
patent.downloadLinkTitlePlay: Play Store link for Android — coming soon
patent.bottomHtml:
🌍 PEACEWAY PAJU × AI interpreting
With this technology, 30+ nationalities on the DMZ peace tour can receive real-time information in their languages from a single guide—and talk across borders in real time.
